description_long: This object is a classic long-barreled firearm with a wooden stock and fore-end, featuring a dark metallic barrel and receiver. The stock is full-sized, smoothly contoured, and made of polished wood, extending into a robust shoulder rest. The barrel is long and slender, with a front sight post and a visible rear sight, and a metallic magazine protrudes from the top, suggesting a bolt-action or similar loading mechanism. The trigger guard is rounded and metallic, encasing a single trigger, and the bolt handle is visible on the right side above the trigger area. The overall finish is a combination of matte wood grain and dark, slightly glossy metal. This shotgun is designed for two-handed operation and is typical of those found in historical or utilitarian urban settings.
description: A long, wood-stocked shotgun with a dark metal barrel, bolt-action mechanism, and a rounded trigger guard. The design is classic and robust, with a full-length wooden stock and metallic magazine.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full wooden stock, long barrel, metallic magazine, and bolt handle detail.
description_view_1: Front-on view highlighting the narrow barrel profile and the alignment of the stock and barrel.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the full length of the shotgun, wooden stock, and metallic components.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the butt of the stock and the straight alignment of the barrel.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, long and slender with no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized metallic guard with smooth edges and no finger grooves
